stem.rawText:
AВ–ВғВҗВүВҮВҗВ–В–В‘В–ВҠВҮ ВӢВ” ВҺВҮвҖ« Э”вҖ¬а¬¶ аө… вҖ« Э•вҖ¬а¬¶ аөҢ 144В’ВғВ•В•ВҮВ•В–ВҠВ”В‘В—ВүВҠВ–ВҠВҮВ’В‘ВӢВҗВ–бҲә20ЗЎ 0бҲ»ВғВҗВҶ В”В‘В•В•ВҮВ•В–ВҠВҮ
       В’В‘В•ВӢВ–ВӢВҳВҮвҖ«Э•вҖ¬ЗҰВғВҡВӢВ•ЗӨ

       ВҠВғВ–ВӢВ•В–ВҠВҮВҳВғВҺВ—ВҮВ‘ВҲвҖ«Э•ВҮВҠВ–В•В–ВҮВҮВҸВ–ВҗВҮВүВҗВғВ–ВҮВҠВ–ВҮВ”ВҮВҠВҷВ–ВҗВӢВ‘В’ВҮВҠВ–В–ВғЭ•вҖ¬ЗҰВғВҡВӢВ•З«

solution.rawText:
This calls for a sketch. The circle x2 + y 2 = 144 is centred on the origin and has radius 12:

                                            y

                                        k




                                                                           x
                                            0         12       20




ItвҖҷs not exact, and itвҖҷs not drawn to scale, but itвҖҷs good enough to work with. The blue radius
is perpendicular to the tangent line. We are trying to find the value of k.

Approach 1
We could work out the equation of the tangent line. It has gradient m (which we donвҖҷt know)
and passes through (20, 0), so it has equation y = m(x вҲ’ 20). Since it is tangent to the circle,
solving the two equations simultaneously must give one repeated root.
We have x2 + y 2 = 144 into which we can substitute y = m(x вҲ’ 20) giving

                                                   x2 + m2 (x вҲ’ 20)2 = 144
                          вҮҗвҮ’                x2 + m2 (x2 вҲ’ 40x + 400) = 144
                          вҮҗвҮ’    (m2 + 1)x2 вҲ’ 40m2 x + 400m2 вҲ’ 144 = 0

We want this to have exactly one root for x, so we need the discriminant to equal zero, giving

                           (вҲ’40m2 )2 вҲ’ 4(m2 + 1)(400m2 вҲ’ 144) = 0.

We can take out a factor of 42 :

                               (10m2 )2 вҲ’ (m2 + 1)(100m2 вҲ’ 36) = 0

and then expand the brackets to give

                           100m4 вҲ’ 100m4 + 36m2 вҲ’ 100m2 + 36 = 0

so 64m2 = 36, or 8m = Вұ6. Since m < 0 (as the line intersects the positive y-axis), we have
m = вҲ’ 86 = вҲ’ 34 . Thus the tangent line has equation

                                         y = вҲ’ 43 (x вҲ’ 20)

Substituting in (x, y) = (0, k) gives k = 15, and hence the answer is B.


Version 1.1, March 2019                                                                    Page 8

                                                                                      В© UCLES 2019
 
Test of Mathematics for University Admission, 2017 Paper 1 Solutions


Approach 2
We might notice from the sketch that we have some similar triangles, since the radius meets
the tangent at right angles. If we label all of the vertices and equal angles, we will be able to
describe things more precisely:

                                            y
                                            P
                                        k

                                                    Q



                                                                R
                                                                        x
                                            O        12       20




So the triangles 4OQR, 4P QO and 4P OR are all similar. We also know the lengths OR = 20
and OQ = 12 (as it is a radius). By PythagorasвҖҷs Theorem, applied to 4OQR, we can calculate
QR = 16 (and we might note that 4OQR is a 3-4-5 triangle). We can then use the similarity
of triangles 4P QO and 4OQR to find k:
                                   PO   OR    k    20
                                      =    =вҮ’    =
                                   QO   QR    12   16
giving k = 15, as before.

      Commentary: You might prefer one or other of these approaches. The second is shorter,
      but requires some confidence with geometry and recognising similar triangles.




Version 1.1, March 2019                                                                   Page 9
